title: Incident Response Procedure Update - PagerDuty Escalation Path
body:
INCIDENT RESPONSE PROCEDURE UPDATE
Effective Date: March 1, 2026
Document ID: comp_irp_pd_escalation_2026_001
Document Type: Compliance Note

1. PURPOSE AND SCOPE
This compliance note documents the updated incident response procedure for TechCorp's SaaS platform, specifically establishing the new PagerDuty escalation path. This update ensures that critical incidents are escalated efficiently to appropriate responders and management personnel in accordance with our incident management obligations and service-level commitments.

2. PAGERDUTY ESCALATION POLICY STRUCTURE
Effective immediately, all production incidents are assigned and escalated according to the following three-tiered escalation policy:

Level 1 - Primary On-Call Engineer: The on-call engineer from the primary rotation is the first responder for all triggered incidents. This responder is responsible for initial triage, acknowledgement, and investigation of the incident. Notifications are sent immediately upon incident trigger through the responder's configured contact methods (email, SMS, phone).

Level 2 - Secondary On-Call Engineer: If the primary responder does not acknowledge the incident within 30 minutes of trigger, the incident automatically escalates to the secondary on-call engineer. The secondary responder provides backup support and can assume ownership of the incident response if the primary responder is unavailable or unable to resolve.

Level 3 - Engineering Management: If the secondary responder does not acknowledge the incident within an additional 30 minutes, the incident escalates to the on-call engineering manager. The engineering manager is responsible for incident coordination, additional resource allocation, executive communication, and ensuring resolution of critical issues affecting service availability.

3. ESCALATION TIMING AND PROTOCOLS
Each escalation rule is configured to trigger after 30 minutes of non-acknowledgement by the assigned responder. Upon escalation, PagerDuty aut
